beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check plants
beats.reasoning: Ethan starts Monday morning at full energy; 30 min is enough for a quick routine before heading out on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Drive from Bellevue to Interbay birth center
beats.reasoning: Across-town commute from Bellevue to Interbay; budget 40 min for early-morning traffic on a sunny Monday.

beats.activity: Morning shift at birth center: patient support, documentation, prep
beats.reasoning: Midwife work is demanding and perfectionist-aligned; morning shift covers the 11am FIFA match time, which Ethan chooses to skip—work comes first.

beats.activity: Drive from Interbay to Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Heading to Capitol Hill for lunch and a quick social meetup; 40 min accounts for midday traffic crossing town.

beats.activity: Lunch with close friend at a Capitol Hill café, discussing vinyl finds
beats.reasoning: Ethan values time with close friends; sunny Monday is perfect for a leisurely lunch catch-up in a familiar neighborhood.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby vinyl record shop
beats.reasoning: Quick 15-min walk within Capitol Hill; Ethan's passion for vinyl collecting makes a record hunt a natural midday activity.

beats.activity: Browse vinyl, chat with shop owner, make a purchase
beats.reasoning: Ethan takes time to enjoy his hobby; an hour of browsing and conversation fits his perfectionist, detail-oriented nature.

beats.activity: Drive from Capitol Hill back to Bellevue
beats.reasoning: Heading home; 50 min accounts for late-afternoon cross-town traffic and Light Rail closure (though Ethan drives, not transit).

beats.activity: Home brewing session: prepare ingredients, start fermentation batch
beats.reasoning: Ethan's other passion; sunny, warm early-June weather is ideal for setting up a brew; perfectionist mindset applies to precise measurements and timing.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, water and check on houseplants, listen to vinyl
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home: cooking, plant care (Ethan names every one), and enjoying the new vinyl purchase sets a calm, grounded tone.

beats.activity: Eat dinner, relax on couch with vinyl and a book, early bedtime prep
beats.reasoning: Low-key evening after a full day of work and social time; competitive perfectionist enjoys a quiet Monday to recharge before the week.
7.5-hour sleep; overnight beat spans into Tuesday morning, ending well before 6am as required.

diary:
Woke up early to water my plants—they're looking healthier than last week, which feels like a small win. The drive to Interbay was smooth, and the birth center was calm this morning. I supported a couple through their labor prep, which reminded me why I do this work. There's something grounding about it.

Skipped the World Cup buzz downtown (the bars were probably packed anyway) and drove over to Capitol Hill instead. Grabbed lunch with my friend at Molly Moon's and we geeked out about vinyl finds. Felt good to decompress. Sonic Boom was calling my name, so I wandered in and ended up talking shop with the owner for a while before grabbing an old Steely Dan album I've been hunting for.

Back home by mid-afternoon, I started a new fermentation batch—there's something meditative about prepping the ingredients, watching it all come together. Cooked dinner, played the new record, and settled in early with a book. Felt peaceful. Needed this kind of day.
thought_process: I was ready for a gentler Monday after a busy weekend. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I'm driving, and honestly, knowing the bars would be crowded with World Cup fans made me want to opt out anyway. I chose Capitol Hill over staying at the birth center partly for mental space—midwifery is rewarding but intense. Lunch with my friend felt necessary, and browsing Sonic Boom afterward scratched an itch I didn't know I had. The vinyl purchase was impulsive but justified; I've been searching for that album. Coming home to brew felt like the right rhythm—hands-on, creative, low-stakes. By evening, I was genuinely tired in the good way, the kind that comes from a day that balanced work, connection, and solitude.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
